children.label: A
children.text: is enrolled under the program under part B and entitled to benefits under part A; and

children.label: B
children.text: is not enrolled in a Medicare+Choice plan under part C, an eligible organization under a contract under (or a similar organization operating under a demonstration project authority), an organization with an agreement under section 1395(a)(1)(A) of this title, or a PACE program under .section 1395mm of this titlesection 1395eee of this titlel

children.heading: Secretary’s discretion as to scope of program
children.label: 2 Secretary’s discretion as to scope of program
A
children.text: a geographic area (or areas) that the Secretary designates for purposes of the program, based upon such criteria as the Secretary finds appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: a subgroup (or subgroups) of beneficiaries or individuals and entities furnishing items or services (otherwise eligible to participate in the program), selected on the basis of the number of such participants that the Secretary finds consistent with the effective and efficient implementation of the program;

children.label: C
children.text: an element (or elements) of the program that the Secretary determines to be suitable for implementation; or

children.label: D
children.text: any combination of any of the limits described in subparagraphs (A) through (C).

children.heading: Voluntary receipt of items and services
children.label: 3 Voluntary receipt of items and services
children.text: Items and services shall be furnished to an individual under the demonstration program only at the individual’s election.

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: 4 Agreements
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to enter into agreements with individuals and entities to furnish health care items and services to beneficiaries under the demonstration program.

children.heading: Program standards and criteria
children.label: 5 Program standards and criteria
children.text: The Secretary shall establish performance standards for the demonstration program including, as applicable, standards for quality of health care items and services, cost-effectiveness, beneficiary satisfaction, and such other factors as the Secretary finds appropriate. The eligibility of individuals or entities for the initial award, continuation, and renewal of agreements to provide health care items and services under the program shall be conditioned, at a minimum, on performance that meets or exceeds such standards.

children.heading: Administrative review of decisions affecting individuals and entities furnishing services
children.label: 6 Administrative review of decisions affecting individuals and entities furnishing services
children.text: An individual or entity furnishing services under the demonstration program shall be entitled to a review by the program administrator (or, if the Secretary has not contracted with a program administrator, by the Secretary) of a decision not to enter into, or to terminate, or not to renew, an agreement with the entity to provide health care items or services under the program.

children.heading: Secretary’s review of marketing materials
children.label: 7 Secretary’s review of marketing materials
children.text: An agreement with an individual or entity furnishing services under the demonstration program shall require the individual or entity to guarantee that it will not distribute materials that market items or services under the program without the Secretary’s prior review and approval.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an individual or entity receiving payment from the Secretary under a contract or agreement under the demonstration program shall agree to accept such payment as payment in full, and such payment shall be in lieu of any payments to which the individual or entity would otherwise be entitled under this subchapter.

children.heading: Collection of deductibles and coinsurance
children.label: B Collection of deductibles and coinsurance
children.text: Such individual or entity may collect any applicable deductible or coinsurance amount from a beneficiary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may administer the demonstration program through a contract with a program administrator in accordance with the provisions of this subsection.

children.heading: Scope of program administrator contracts
children.label: 2 Scope of program administrator contracts
children.text: The Secretary may enter into such contracts for a limited geographic area, or on a regional or national basis.

children.label: A
children.text: an entity that, under a contract under section 1395h or 1395u of this title, determines the amount of and makes payments for health care items and services furnished under this subchapter; or

children.label: B
children.text: any other entity with substantial experience in managing the type of program concerned.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A contract under this subsection shall be for an initial term of up to three years, renewable for additional terms of up to three years.

children.heading: Noncompetitive award and renewal for entities administering part A or part B payments
children.label: B Noncompetitive award and renewal for entities administering part A or part B payments
children.text: The Secretary may enter or renew a contract under this subsection with an entity described in paragraph (3)(A) without regard to the requirements of .section 6101 of title 41

children.heading: Applicability of Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.label: 5 Applicability of Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.text: The Federal Acquisition Regulation shall apply to program administration contracts under this subsection.

children.heading: Performance standards
children.label: 6 Performance standards
children.text: The Secretary shall establish performance standards for the program administrator including, as applicable, standards for the quality and cost-effectiveness of the program administered, and such other factors as the Secretary finds appropriate. The eligibility of entities for the initial award, continuation, and renewal of program administration contracts shall be conditioned, at a minimum, on performance that meets or exceeds such standards.

children.heading: Agreements with entities furnishing health care items and services
children.label: A Agreements with entities furnishing health care items and services
children.text: Determine the qualifications of entities seeking to enter or renew agreements to provide services under the demonstration program, and as appropriate enter or renew (or refuse to enter or renew) such agreements on behalf of the Secretary.

children.heading: Establishment of payment rates
children.label: B Establishment of payment rates
children.text: Negotiate or otherwise establish, subject to the Secretary’s approval, payment rates for covered health care items and services.

children.heading: Payment of claims or fees
children.label: C Payment of claims or fees
children.text: Administer payments for health care items or services furnished under the program.

children.heading: Payment of bonuses
children.label: D Payment of bonuses
children.text: Using such guidelines as the Secretary shall establish, and subject to the approval of the Secretary, make bonus payments as described in subsection (c)(2)(B) to entities furnishing items or services for which payment may be made under the program.

children.heading: Oversight
children.label: E Oversight
children.text: Monitor the compliance of individuals and entities with agreements under the program with the conditions of participation.

children.heading: Administrative review
children.label: F Administrative review
children.text: Conduct reviews of adverse determinations specified in subsection (a)(6).

children.heading: Review of marketing materials
children.label: G Review of marketing materials
children.text: Conduct a review of marketing materials proposed by an entity furnishing services under the program.

children.heading: Additional functions
children.label: H Additional functions
children.text: Perform such other functions as the Secretary may specify.

children.heading: Limitation of liability
children.label: 8 Limitation of liability
children.text: The provisions of shall apply with respect to activities of contractors and their officers, employees, and agents under a contract under this subsection.section 1320c–6(b) of this title

children.heading: Information sharing
children.label: 9 Information sharing
children.text: Notwithstanding and , the Secretary is authorized to disclose to an entity with a program administration contract under this subsection such information (including medical information) on individuals receiving health care items and services under the program as the entity may require to carry out its responsibilities under the contract.section 1306 of this titlesection 552a of title 5

children.heading: Rules applicable to both program agreements and program administration contracts
children.label: c Rules applicable to both program agreements and program administration contracts

children.heading: Records, reports, and audits
children.label: 1 Records, reports, and audits
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to require entities with agreements to provide health care items or services under the demonstration program, and entities with program administration contracts under subsection (b), to maintain adequate records, to afford the Secretary access to such records (including for audit purposes), and to furnish such reports and other materials (including audited financial statements and performance data) as the Secretary may require for purposes of implementation, oversight, and evaluation of the program and of individuals’ and entities’ effectiveness in performance of such agreements or contracts.

children.heading: Payments to program administrators
children.label: A Payments to program administrators
children.text: The Secretary may make bonus payments under the program to program administrators.

children.heading: Payments to entities furnishing services
children.label: B Payments to entities furnishing services

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary may make bonus payments to individuals or entities furnishing items or services for which payment may be made under the demonstration program, or may authorize the program administrator to make such bonus payments in accordance with such guidelines as the Secretary shall establish and subject to the Secretary’s approval.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: ii Limitations
children.text: The Secretary may condition such payments on the achievement of such standards related to efficiency, improvement in processes or outcomes of care, or such other factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Antidiscrimination limitation
children.label: 3 Antidiscrimination limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not enter into an agreement with an entity to provide health care items or services under the demonstration program, or with an entity to administer the program, unless such entity guarantees that it will not deny, limit, or condition the coverage or provision of benefits under the program, for individuals eligible to be enrolled under such program, based on any health status-related factor described in section 2702(a)(1) of the Public Health Service Act.1See References in Text note below.1
Limiting the implementation of the program under subsection (a)(2).

Establishment of program participation standards under subsection (a)(5) or the denial or termination of, or refusal to renew, an agreement with an entity to provide health care items and services under the program.

Establishment of program administration contract performance standards under subsection (b)(6), the refusal to renew a program administration contract, or the noncompetitive award or renewal of a program administration contract under subsection (b)(4)(B).
Establishment of payment rates, through negotiation or otherwise, under a program agreement or a program administration contract.

children.label: A
children.text: as to whether cost savings have been achieved, and the amount of savings; or

children.label: B
children.text: as to whether, to whom, and in what amounts bonuses will be paid.

children.heading: Application limited to parts A and B
children.label: e Application limited to parts A and B
children.text: None of the provisions of this section or of the demonstration program shall apply to the programs under part C.

children.heading: Reports to Congress
children.label: f Reports to Congress
children.text: Not later than two years after , and biennially thereafter for six years, the Secretary shall report to Congress on the use of authorities under the demonstration program. Each report shall address the impact of the use of those authorities on expenditures, access, and quality under the programs under this subchapter.December 21, 2000
